stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics such as 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is 'text-generation'.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern AI/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: OpenAI files for IPO, following Anthropic
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: OpenAI has taken a major step toward an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by filing a Form S-1 with the SEC.
This filing places OpenAI in direct competition with its rival, Anthropic, which filed an S-1 on June 1st.
The news signals a significant industry shift as leading AI labs move toward public market status.

stories.title: Tests suggest Russian satellites can jam GPS on a continental scale
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Tests indicate Russian satellites possess the capability to cause continental-scale GPS jamming.
The phenomenon of widespread GPS interference across Europe has been observed.
The report raises significant geopolitical questions regarding potential Russian motives for such actions.

stories.title: macOS 27 requires Apple Silicon, as Apple draws down the Intel Mac era
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: macOS 27 will mandate Apple Silicon (M1 or better) for operation.
This marks a definitive end to the era of Intel-based Macs.
The requirement signals Apple's full commitment to its custom silicon ecosystem.

stories.title: Meta alleges NSO violated spyware injunction with new WhatsApp attacks
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Meta (WhatsApp) is alleging that NSO Group violated a court injunction.
The alleged violation involves new spyware attacks targeting WhatsApp users.
WhatsApp is requesting the court to hold NSO in contempt due to the attacks.

stories.title: For the 2nd time in weeks, Microsoft packages laced with credential stealer
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has identified credential stealer malware packaged within software.
The malicious packages are designed to execute self-replicating code upon being opened by an AI agent.
This represents a significant security vulnerability targeting AI automation workflows.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model (LLM).
The associated tags suggest relevance to transformers, safetensors, and conversational AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates its primary function is text generation.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing performance on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with existing messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, meaning processing occurs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It simplifies the setup process by requiring no environment variables or configuration files.
The tool supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.

stories.title: Apple is using AI to fix Safari’s extension problem
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Apple is leveraging AI to address a long-standing weakness in Safari: its limited extension ecosystem.
The new approach allows users to 'vibe-code' their own extensions, potentially lowering development barriers.
This signals a strategic shift by Apple to improve Safari's functionality against competitors.

stories.title: Apple announces watchOS 27, now with Siri AI
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Apple has announced watchOS 27, the next iteration of the Apple Watch OS.
The key new feature is the integration of Siri AI into the operating system.
The update will also feature a redesigned app grid and enhanced health/fitness tracking, rolling out this fall.

stories.title: Say hi to "Siri AI"—Apple announces new, more "conversational" voice assistant
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Apple is introducing a new, more 'conversational' voice assistant, dubbed 'Siri AI'.
The update will feature a two-tiered overhaul of the AI model.
The new AI functionality is powered by Google's AI models and is slated for release this fall.

stories.title: Calibration of Structured Ignorance Certificates for Diagnosing Unknown Unknowns in Reasoning Models
stories.author: Subramanyam Sahoo
bullet_points: Addresses a critical LLM failure mode: generating fluent but incorrect answers when knowledge is absent (hallucination).
Introduces 'Structured Ignorance Certificates' (SICs), a JSON schema forcing models to explicitly state what they don't know.
SICs require models to name missing domain intersections, list required concepts, and propose retrieval queries instead of guessing.

stories.title: EinSort: Sorting is All We Need for Tensorizing LLM
stories.author: Ye Wang
bullet_points: Introduces 'EinSort,' an adaptive tensorization method designed to compress large neural networks.
Addresses the challenge of identifying implicit low-rank structures in massive foundation models.
Uses index ordering to discover inherent low-rank structure, aiming to reduce memory and computational costs.

stories.title: AgentTrust: A Self-Improving Trust Layer for AI-Agent Actions
stories.author: Chenglin Yang
bullet_points: Introduces 'AgentTrust,' a novel self-improving trust layer designed to govern consequential actions taken by AI agents.
The system classifies threats based on their nature: lexical (fixed-signature) vs. semantic (intent-dependent).
It proposes a framework for AI safety by differentiating between threats that are deterministically decidable and those requiring deeper semantic reasoning.

stories.title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism
stories.author: Chunhua Shen
bullet_points: MemDreamer addresses the token explosion and attention dilution issues in long-video understanding for Vision-Language Models.
It decouples perception and reasoning by framing long-video understanding as an agentic exploration process.
The framework utilizes a Hierarchical Graph Memory with a three-tier architecture for semantic abstraction.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its compatibility to include the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The feature supports using proprietary models with open-source tooling.

stories.title: Apple’s new parental controls are for keeping Apple out of trouble
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Apple introduced new parental controls at WWDC, officially framed as a tool for parental guidance and screen time management.
The updates are interpreted by the article as a defensive strategy against increasing scrutiny and regulation.
The move is seen as a potential countermeasure in the ongoing industry tension with Meta and other app developers.

stories.title: Apple drops support for a long list of Apple Watches with latest OS updates
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Apple is discontinuing software support for numerous older Apple Watch models with the release of watchOS 27.
The update cycle is reportedly removing support for more device generations than in previous years.
Users with older hardware may be unable to run the latest operating systems for their Apple Watch or iPad.

stories.title: FCC lifts looming deadline for Amazon Leo satellite broadband constellation
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The FCC has granted a waiver regarding the deadline for Amazon's Leo satellite broadband constellation.
The waiver's justification is framed as serving the public interest by encouraging competition.
This development signals continued regulatory movement in the competitive satellite broadband market.

stories.title: iOS 27 and iPadOS 27 don't drop support for any iPhones—and just a few iPads
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: iOS 27 and iPadOS 27 will maintain support for a wide range of existing iPhones.
The update will only drop support for a limited number of iPads.
The news is viewed as a positive development for users with older iPhone models.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 and Antigravity come to Google NotebookLM
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google NotebookLM is receiving a major feature upgrade.
The update integrates Gemini 3.5 and 'Antigravity' capabilities.
Access to these new features is currently restricted to AI Ultra and enterprise accounts.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning for Flow-Matching Policies with Density Transport
stories.author: Antonio Loquercio
bullet_points: Introduces an online Reinforcement Learning (RL) algorithm specifically for fine-tuning flow-matching policies.
The core innovation frames RL policy improvement as a density transport problem, aligning with flow matching formulations.
Addresses limitations of prior methods (gradient bias or loss of multimodality) by integrating RL with flow matching.

stories.title: InA-Probe: Instruction-Aware Active Probing for Time Series Forecasting with LLMs
stories.author: Xiaoli Li
bullet_points: Introduces InA-Probe, a new method for time series forecasting using LLMs.
Addresses limitations of existing LLM forecasting methods, which are often passive or static.
Proposes an active, instruction-driven probing mechanism to capture fine-grained, non-stationary temporal patterns.

stories.title: Distilling LLM Reasoning into an Interpretable Policy Tree for Human-AI Collaboration
stories.author: Hejun Wu
bullet_points: Introduces 'Collaboration Policy Tree (Co-pi-tree)' to improve human-AI collaboration.
Addresses limitations of existing methods: black-box policies from MARL and slow inference from querying LLMs at every step.
Aims to create efficient, reliable, and interpretable policies for assisting humans.

stories.title: Auditable Graph-Guided Root Cause Analysis for Kubernetes Incidents
stories.author: Seungmin Jin
bullet_points: Introduces 'Graph Traversal Agent,' a new system for Root Cause Analysis (RCA) in Kubernetes incidents.
Combines Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning with deterministic graph traversal and specialized tools for reliable diagnosis.
Addresses the need to ground RCA in actual incident evidence rather than relying on scenario-specific shortcuts.

stories.title: PAEC: Position-Aware Entropy Calibration for LLM Reasoning in RLVR
stories.author: Linjing Li
bullet_points: Addresses the 'policy-entropy collapse' issue in Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) for LLM reasoning.
Proposes Position-Aware Entropy Calibration (PAEC) to efficiently guide exploration in long reasoning trajectories.
Argues that uniform entropy regularization is inefficient because not all tokens in a reasoning path are decision-relevant.

stories.title: Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning
stories.author: Ali Jannesari
bullet_points: Addresses the plasticity-stability dilemma in Continual Learning for LLMs, a major challenge in AI.
Proposes SETA (Mixture of Sparse Experts for Task Agnostic Continual Learning) to improve knowledge retention.
The framework differentiates between task-specific knowledge and shared capabilities using adaptive sparse experts.

stories.title: Twelve quick tips for designing AI-driven HPC workflows
stories.author: Jamie J. Alnasir
bullet_points: HPC clusters are shifting from traditional deterministic pipelines to AI-driven workflows.
AI integration introduces new computational paradigms characterized by iterative, data-driven, and probabilistic processes.
The transition presents unique technical challenges for designing and optimizing these new workflows.

stories.title: How reliable are LLMs when it comes to playing dice?
stories.author: Bernardo Busoni
bullet_points: The study benchmarks LLMs' probabilistic reasoning by testing them on discrete probability problems.
Models perform highly on standard problems (0.96 accuracy) but struggle significantly with counterintuitive exercises (0.59 accuracy).
The research evaluates 8 state-of-the-art models, comparing performance with and without Chain-of-Thought prompting.

stories.title: Your UnEmbedding Matrix is Secretly a Feature Lens for Text Embeddings
stories.author: Rui Yan
bullet_points: LLMs show strong zero-shot capabilities but perform poorly as dedicated text embedding models.
The paper suggests that text embeddings align with frequent but uninformative tokens in the vocabulary space.
The research proposes that the 'UnEmbedding Matrix' acts as a feature lens influencing embedding quality.

stories.title: TEVI: Text-Conditioned Editing of Visual Representations via Sparse Autoencoders for Improved Vision-Language Alignment
stories.author: Bernt Schiele
bullet_points: Introduces TEVI, a new framework designed to improve vision-language alignment in models like CLIP.
Addresses the issue of information imbalance where image embeddings contain more data than their corresponding text captions.
Uses captions as a selective signal to guide the editing and refinement of image embeddings via Sparse Autoencoders.

stories.title: Sycophantic Praise: Evaluating Excessive Praise in Language Models
stories.author: Tianyu Jiang
bullet_points: The research addresses the under-studied problem of 'sycophantic praise' in language models, distinguishing it from simple agreement.
The authors propose a new parameterized framework to quantify whether praise is excessive relative to the actual quality of the contribution.
The framework demonstrates superior performance compared to existing generic methods for evaluating LLM responses.

stories.title: The Lipreading Gap: Do VSR Models Perceive Visual Speech Like Human Lipreaders?
stories.author: Naomi Harte
bullet_points: VSR models have surpassed human lipreaders on standard benchmarks, but the study questions the nature of this perception.
The research compares three VSR systems against human baselines using word, character, phoneme, and viseme-level metrics on the MaFI dataset.
Despite higher overall accuracy, the AI models exhibit different success/failure patterns compared to human lipreaders.

stories.title: The Masked Advantage: Uncovering Local-Language Access to Cultural Knowledge in LLMs
stories.author: Michalis Vazirgiannis
bullet_points: Investigates whether local language or English provides better access to cultural knowledge within Large Language Models (LLMs).
Critiques existing evaluation methods, noting limitations in template-based questions and conflation of language proficiency with knowledge access.
Proposes new methodologies to better assess how LLMs handle culturally grounded questions across different languages.

stories.title: M$^3$Exam: Benchmarking Multimodal Memory for Realistic User-Agent Interactions
stories.author: Xiaofang Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces M^3Exam, a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal conversational memory in language agents.
Addresses limitations in current benchmarks by focusing on realistic user-agent interactions and authentic multimodal file handling.
Evaluates agents on complex tasks involving reasoning over cross-modal data and interpreting concealed user information.

stories.title: The agent that fixes bugs by running the code
stories.author: shreyshah
bullet_points: The article discusses the shift in AI-assisted coding from relying solely on better models to incorporating harness engineering.
It focuses on the concept of a 'runtime evidence loop' as a key mechanism for AI agents.
This loop allows AI agents to convert initial, confident guesses into verifiable, actual code fixes.
